source_pr.title: executor: speed up TestEarlyClose
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->

`TestEarlyClose` is too slow.
For the reference, this test is introduced in 0e61c8591859c361d5e9fb2a2b90fe1a6c35c55d.

### What is changed and how it works?


before:
```
go test -race -check.f TestEarlyClose

OK: 1 passed
PASS
ok  	github.com/pingcap/tidb/executor/seqtest	22.775s
```

after:
```
go test -race -check.f TestEarlyClose

OK: 1 passed
PASS
ok  	github.com/pingcap/tidb/executor/seqtest	2.436s
```

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - No code

symptoms: TestEarlyClose is slow under `-race` (large row counts / many iterations), which can contribute to timeout-like instability in CI.
failure_signature: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: No concrete flaky failure signature is provided; the patch primarily reduces test workload (N/loop count) to speed up execution.
root_cause_explanation: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE: This PR mainly speeds up `TestEarlyClose` by reducing the number of inserted rows and iterations. While that can help CI stability, the available case evidence does not include a concrete intermittent failure signature or a targeted determinism fix, so avoid guessing a nondeterminism root cause and keep it as needs-more-evidence.
fix_pattern: N/A (insufficient evidence; no targeted stabilization change identified).
analysis:

Triage summary: go test -race -check.f TestEarlyClose
Changed files (1): executor/seqtest/seq_executor_test.go
Patch url: https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/pull/13386.patch
Picked test: type=unit path=executor/seqtest/seq_executor_test.go name=TestEarlyClose
